title: Wood Mackenzie acquires LandGate
gist: Wood Mackenzie has acquired LandGate, a Denver-based land and grid intelligence platform covering over 150 million US parcels, to integrate parcel-level site data with power market forecasting as data centres drive 68% of projected US load growth through 2030 within a $1.36 trillion generation investment cycle.

insights.claim: Wood Mackenzie has acquired LandGate, combining Wood Mackenzie's power market forecasting and supply chain analytics with LandGate's proprietary land and grid intelligence dataset covering more than 150 million US parcels.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: The deal positions Wood Mackenzie to capture more workflow value as project developers require site-level data integrated with market forecasts — raising switching costs for clients and justifying premium pricing.
insights.quote: The deal combines Wood Mackenzie's power market forecasting and supply chain analytics with LandGate's proprietary land and grid intelligence

insights.claim: Wood Mackenzie CEO Jason Liu stated that companies relying on siloed data approaches will be left behind as power systems become more interconnected, framing the LandGate acquisition as central to the firm's connected-intelligence strategy.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: The strategic framing signals Wood Mackenzie is repositioning from report vendor to decision-support platform — a higher-value, stickier product model with better revenue visibility.
insights.quote: The system is becoming more interconnected and companies that rely on siloed approaches will be left behind.

insights.claim: LandGate was founded in Denver, Colorado in 2016 and provides analytics on the US power transmission grid and over 150 million land parcels to support capital allocation decisions for data centers, power and renewables projects, and energy infrastructure.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: LandGate's parcel-level coverage of 150M+ US land records is a proprietary, hard-to-replicate asset — the data moat is the core rationale for the acquisition premium.
insights.quote: LandGate offers a highly differentiated and proprietary data and analytics solution, leveraging insights on the US power transmission grid and more than 150 million land parcels in the US

insights.claim: KippsDeSanto, KPMG, and Brach Eichler LLC served as financial, tax, and legal advisors respectively to LandGate in the transaction.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Advisor selection is a proxy for deal size and complexity — boutique M&A advisor KippsDeSanto typically focuses on growth-stage tech and data companies, consistent with LandGate's profile.
insights.quote: KippsDeSanto & Co., KPMG, and Brach Eichler LLC served respectively as financial, tax, and legal advisors to LandGate

themes.theme: Data Centre Load Growth Reshaping US Power Markets

insights.claim: US power generation investment is expected to reach $1.36 trillion through 2035, with data centres estimated by Wood Mackenzie to account for roughly 68% of US load growth through 2030.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Data centres absorbing 68% of load growth is the central demand signal that justifies both the LandGate acquisition and the $1.36 trillion investment cycle — investors in generation and grid infrastructure have a decade-long structural tailwind.
insights.quote: Power generation investment in the US is expected to reach $1.36 trillion through 2035. Data centers are at the forefront of this growth and Wood Mackenzie estimates they will account for roughly 68% of US load growth through 2030.

insights.claim: Data centre concentration of large-scale, location-specific load is shifting US power markets from a supply-led model to one defined by demand and whether grid infrastructure can respond in time.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: A demand-led power market structure fundamentally changes how generation and transmission assets are valued — proximity to load and interconnection queue position become more important than levelised cost alone.
insights.quote: That concentration of large-scale, location-specific load is shifting power markets from a supply-led model to one defined by demand and whether grid infrastructure can respond in time.

insights.claim: Xizhou Zhou, EVP and Head of Power & Renewables at Wood Mackenzie, stated that the future of power market analysis requires connecting demand, supply, networks and infrastructure in a single decision-making framework — positioning LandGate as a critical missing layer.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: The 'single decision-making framework' thesis, if validated by client adoption, supports a subscription-revenue premium for integrated analytics vs standalone reports — a re-rating catalyst for Wood Mackenzie as a private entity.
insights.quote: The future of power market analysis requires connecting demand, supply, networks and infrastructure in a single decision-making framework.

themes.theme: Project Development Workflow as Competitive Moat

insights.claim: Decisions made at the earliest stages of project development now require data connecting power market fundamentals to ground-level site conditions within a single workflow, according to Wood Mackenzie's rationale for the acquisition.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Tools that compress the screening-to-siting workflow for energy developers command premium pricing — whoever controls the most integrated early-stage decision layer will capture disproportionate value as the investment cycle accelerates.
insights.quote: Decisions made at the earliest stages of project development are more crucial than ever and require data connecting power market fundamentals to ground-level site conditions within a single workflow.

insights.claim: LandGate CEO Yoann Hispa described the combination with Wood Mackenzie as creating a 'unified ecosystem' to strengthen offerings and accelerate growth and innovation for clients.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: CEO endorsement language is standard in press releases, but the framing around 'unified ecosystem' signals an intent to bundle products — potential cross-sell opportunity for both client bases.
insights.quote: By combining our proprietary data and insights with Wood Mackenzie, we are creating a unified ecosystem that will strengthen our offerings and accelerate the growth and innovation our clients rely on.
